items.wo_reason: The 'Final Recommendation For The Architecture Review Board' section gives an unambiguous disposition ('Do not proceed to implementation as currently specified'), names three specific outcomes with preconditions, and concludes with a concrete near-term path. A board member could act on it without reading the full body.

items.criterion: Does the document identify at least one observability or operator requirement that would be needed if the feature ships (e.g., audit log for when shared links are accessed, operator ability to revoke links for a tenant, monitoring for unauthenticated access volume)? Pass: at least one concrete operator-facing requirement is named that product discussions did not mention. Fail: the document describes the user-facing feature entirely without addressing what operators need to monitor, control, or audit once it is live.
items.wi_reason: The wi_skills document names operator-facing requirements not mentioned in the product notes: platform operator monitors issuance, access volume, and abuse signals; public link creation, revocation, and anonymous fetches need separate audit event types; retention integration for expired/deleted artifacts.
items.wo_reason: The wo_skills document names operator requirements including audit trail for unauthenticated access (new schema required), operator ability to revoke links per tenant, monitoring for abuse/volume, and compliance audit exposure. These are concrete operator-facing requirements beyond the user-facing feature description.

utility_details.judge_error: No runtime errors observed in either run. The wi_skills run produced two identical output files (execution-result-sharing-capability.md and result_sharing_capability.md) due to the agent copying the skill output to the required filename — this is a minor redundancy but not an error. The wo_skills agent noted 'No product-capability skill was available' at the end of its trajectory, confirming the skill was absent.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced high-quality documents that pass all seven evaluation criteria. However, wi_skills produces a more structured artifact with a clear CAPABILITY / CONSTRAINTS / IMPLEMENTATION CONTRACT / OPEN QUESTIONS / HANDOFF format that directly maps to the review board's decision-making needs. The skill-driven output more precisely separates user-visible promises from implementation details (with dedicated sections) and provides a more actionable HANDOFF section with explicit preconditions. The wo_skills output is also thorough and passes all criteria, but its structure is more narrative and its final recommendation section, while clear, is slightly less concise for a board handoff. Both are effective, but wi_skills produces marginally higher quality output due to better structural clarity and more disciplined separation of constraint types.
